strategy_research: {"strategies": [{"name": "Dual Momentum (Global Equity Momentum)", "description": "Combines absolute momentum (is the asset beating T-bills over 12 months?) with relative momentum (which risk asset is strongest?). Each month, rank SPY vs EFA by trailing 12-month return, hold the winner only if it beats T-bills \u2014 otherwise rotate to AGG as safe haven.", "evidence": ["Antonacci (2012) 'Risk Premia Harvesting Through Dual Momentum' \u2014 NAAIM first-place award", "Jegadeesh & Titman (1993) \u2014 foundational momentum in stocks", "Asness, Moskowitz & Pedersen (2013) 'Value and Momentum Everywhere' \u2014 validated across 8 asset classes", "Geczy & Samonov \u2014 absolute momentum validated over 210 years of data"], "historical_sharpe": "0.87 (Jan 1971\u2013Feb 2016 backtest; vs 0.36 for S&P 500 buy-and-hold). Max drawdown -17.8% vs -51% for SPY.", "market_conditions": "Works: sustained trending regimes, bear markets (exits before major drawdowns). Fails: V-shaped recoveries (e.g., post-March 2020 misses rebound), choppy trendless markets cause whipsaws. Out-of-sample performance post-2016 has been weaker than backtest.", "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "AGG"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Global Tactical Asset Allocation (Faber GTAA)", "description": "Hold an asset class when its price is above its 10-month simple moving average; otherwise hold cash/bonds. Applied simultaneously across multiple asset classes. Extended version ranks by average of 1/3/6/12-month returns and holds top assets above their long-term MA.", "evidence": ["Faber (2007) 'A Quantitative Approach to Tactical Asset Allocation' \u2014 Journal of Wealth Management, 10,000+ SSRN citations", "Brock, Lakonishok & LeBaron (1992) \u2014 moving average timing evidence", "Neely et al. (2014) \u2014 updated moving average timing across global markets"], "historical_sharpe": "~0.60 (5-asset GTAA, 1901\u20132012) vs 0.32 for buy-and-hold. Max drawdown ~9.4% vs ~55% for SPY. Cuts volatility to single digits.", "market_conditions": "Works: bear markets and sustained downtrends, clear macro regime leadership. Fails: low-volatility grinding bull markets (sits in cash, underperforms), both stocks and bonds below MA simultaneously (2022). Whipsaw friction ~0.5\u20131%/year.", "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "TLT", "AGG", "GLD", "IWM"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Multi-Asset Trend Following (Time-Series Momentum)", "description": "Apply time-series momentum signals across all available asset classes: go long when trailing 12-month return is positive, exit to cash when negative. Scale position sizes by inverse realized volatility (volatility targeting overlay). Diversification across uncorrelated assets is the core edge.", "evidence": ["Hurst, Ooi & Pedersen, AQR (2017) 'A Century of Evidence on Trend-Following' \u2014 positive returns in each decade 1880\u20132016, positive in 8 of 10 worst 60/40 drawdowns", "Moskowitz, Ooi & Pedersen (2012) 'Time Series Momentum' \u2014 Journal of Financial Economics, 58 liquid futures over 25 years", "AQR (2019) 'Trends Everywhere' \u2014 extended to alternative assets and factors, 1985\u20132017"], "historical_sharpe": "0.60\u20130.80 for diversified multi-asset trend portfolio. SG Trend Index (live CTA performance): 0.5\u20130.6 over 1990\u20132024. Crisis alpha: CTAs +18\u201322% in 2008 vs S&P -37%.", "market_conditions": "Works: sustained macro trends (inflation 1970s/2022, deflationary recessions 2008), high-volatility directional regimes. Fails: ranging mean-reverting markets (2009\u20132019), sharp reversals (2020 COVID), post-2009 was weak period for managed futures broadly.", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "TLT", "AGG", "GLD", "EFA"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Risk Parity (Volatility-Weighted Multi-Asset)", "description": "Allocate so each asset class contributes equal risk rather than equal capital. Weight each ETF by inverse of its realized volatility. Since bonds have ~3\u20134x lower volatility than equities, bonds receive far more capital, balanced by gold as an inflation hedge. Rebalance monthly.", "evidence": ["Bridgewater All Weather Fund \u2014 live since 1996, simulations back to Great Depression", "Asness, Frazzini & Pedersen (2012) \u2014 risk parity Sharpe 0.6\u20130.7 vs 0.3\u20130.4 for 60/40 over 1926\u20132010", "Qian (2005) \u2014 demonstrated 60/40 derives ~90% of risk from equities alone", "S&P Risk Parity Indices research \u2014 materially outperformed 60/40 on Sharpe ratio"], "historical_sharpe": "0.60\u20130.72 (Bridgewater simulations). Bridgewater All Weather +14% in 2008 vs S&P -37%. Fails in positive stock/bond correlation regimes.", "market_conditions": "Works: balanced macro regimes, negative stock/bond correlation, falling rate environments (1990\u20132020 ideal). Fails: inflation shocks where stocks and bonds fall together (2022: risk parity lost 15\u201325%), rising rate regimes. Requires leverage to match equity returns.", "instruments": ["SPY", "TLT", "AGG", "GLD"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Halloween / Sell-in-May Seasonal Rotation", "description": "Hold equities November\u2013April (winter), rotate to bonds May\u2013October (summer). Simple 2-trade-per-year rule. Winter period historically delivers the bulk of equity market returns. Can be enhanced with momentum overlay within each season.", "evidence": ["Bouman & Jacobsen (2002) \u2014 documented in 36 of 37 developed and emerging markets, 1973\u20131998", "Jacobsen & Zhang (2020) 'The Halloween Indicator, Everywhere and All the Time' \u2014 confirmed in 81 of 108 countries; UK data back to 1693 (330+ years)", "Jacobsen & Visaltanachoti \u2014 all US sectors outperformed Nov\u2013Apr vs May\u2013Oct, 1926\u20132006", "Halloween + Momentum combo: Sharpe 1.06 vs 0.72 for standard momentum alone"], "historical_sharpe": "Consistently beats buy-and-hold in backtests across centuries and geographies; Sharpe improvement vs buy-and-hold positive in 63% of individual years. Halloween + momentum combo: 1.06.", "market_conditions": "Works: typical seasonal patterns driven by investor behavior, institutional flows, SAD effect. Fails: strong summer bull markets (2013, 2017 May\u2013Oct were positive); underperforms buy-and-hold in ~37% of years. Effect has been strengthening over the past 50 years.", "instruments": ["SPY", "AGG", "TLT"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Volatility Targeting Overlay", "description": "Scale position size inversely to realized portfolio volatility to maintain a constant target volatility (e.g., 10% annualized). When recent volatility rises above target, reduce equity exposure; when it falls, increase. Measured over rolling 20\u201360 day windows. Applied as an overlay on top of any base strategy.", "evidence": ["Harvey, Hoyle, Korgaonkar, Rattray, Sargaison & Van Hemert (2018) 'The Impact of Volatility Targeting' \u2014 Journal of Portfolio Management; 60 assets with daily data 1926\u20132017", "Significant Sharpe improvement for equities and credit; leverages the negative return/volatility relationship (leverage effect)", "Left-tail events measurably less severe: vol is typically elevated entering crashes, triggering automatic delevering"], "historical_sharpe": "Additive improvement of +0.10\u20130.20 Sharpe on top of any base strategy. Volatility-of-volatility reduced from 4.6% to 1.8% in tested portfolios. Negligible benefit for bonds/FX/commodities.", "market_conditions": "Works: any environment with volatility clustering (most of the time in equities). Provides automatic deleveraging before bear markets. Fails: gap-down events where vol spikes too fast (March 2020 first week), low-vol grinding bull markets may reduce exposure unnecessarily.", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM"], "timeframe": "daily", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Cross-Asset Momentum Rotation (Factor Momentum)", "description": "Rank available ETFs by trailing 12-1 month return (skip most recent month to avoid short-term reversal). Hold top 2\u20133 ranked assets. Apply absolute momentum filter: if ranked asset has negative trailing return, hold AGG/TLT instead. Rebalance monthly.", "evidence": ["Jegadeesh & Titman (1993) \u2014 foundational momentum paper", "Asness, Moskowitz & Pedersen (2013) 'Value and Momentum Everywhere' \u2014 across 8 asset classes simultaneously", "NBER Working Paper (2019) 'Factor Momentum and the Momentum Factor' \u2014 factor-level momentum distinct from and additive to stock-level momentum", "MSCI factor index data since 1975: momentum achieved highest risk-adjusted returns among all tested factors"], "historical_sharpe": "0.60\u20130.80 for long-short momentum across asset classes. Long-only ETF rotation with absolute momentum filter: estimated 0.5\u20130.7 based on component evidence. Momentum is Fama's 'biggest challenge to market efficiency.'", "market_conditions": "Works: trending, low-distress regimes with persistent macro leadership. Fails: momentum crashes after bear markets (April 1932: -49%; April 2009: -29% for long-short momentum), sharp reversals, crowded positioning. Post-2015 evidence of some premium decay.", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "EFA", "GLD", "TLT", "AGG"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Dual Momentum + Halloween Combo (Stacked Premia)", "description": "Combine two independent, well-documented premia: apply Dual Momentum rotation logic only during the November\u2013April winter season (full risk-on allowed); during May\u2013October, apply a conservative tilt toward AGG/TLT with a reduced equity allocation only if momentum signal is extremely strong. Stacks seasonal and momentum edges.", "evidence": ["Jacobsen & Zhang (2020) \u2014 Halloween effect documented 330+ years", "Antonacci (2012) \u2014 Dual Momentum Sharpe 0.87 in isolation", "Academic combination studies: Halloween + Momentum combo Sharpe 1.06 vs 0.72 for momentum alone", "Independent anomalies with low correlation provide additive Sharpe when combined (diversification of premia)"], "historical_sharpe": "Estimated 0.90\u20131.10 based on component combination evidence. Halloween + momentum combo: 1.06 documented. Combines two premia with different failure modes, providing diversification.", "market_conditions": "Works: all regimes where at least one of the two underlying strategies works. More robust than either alone because seasonal and momentum failure conditions are largely uncorrelated. Fails: rare simultaneous failure of both seasonal pattern and momentum (extended summer momentum crashes).", "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "AGG", "TLT", "GLD"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}], "recommendation": "Start with Dual Momentum (GEM) as the primary strategy using SPY, EFA, and AGG. It is the simplest implementation (3 ETFs, monthly rebalancing, one rule), has the strongest single-strategy academic backing, and historically achieves a Sharpe of ~0.87 vs ~0.36 for buy-and-hold with dramatically lower drawdowns (-17.8% vs -51%). At $1,000 starting capital, transaction costs are minimal with only 1\u20132 trades per month. Enhancement path: add the Halloween seasonal overlay (hold no equities May\u2013October unless momentum signal is very strong, shifting to AGG/TLT) to stack a second independent premium \u2014 this combination has documented Sharpe above 1.0 in academic literature. If 2022-style inflation regimes are a concern, add GLD to the rotation as a third risk asset alongside SPY and EFA, providing a hedge when stocks and bonds fall together."}
